Content:
# unicode-animations

Unicode spinner animations as raw frame data — no dependencies, works everywhere.

## Demo

See all 18 spinners animating live:
Translation:
# unicode-animations

Unicode spinner animations as raw frame data — no dependencies, works everywhere.

## Demo

See all 18 spinners animating live:

RowTranslations.originContent: Unicode spinner animations as raw frame data — no dependencies, works everywhere.
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Unicode spinner animations as raw frame data — no dependencies, works everywhere.

Content:
```bash
npx unicode-animations --web     # open browser demo
npx unicode-animations           # cycle through all in terminal
npx unicode-animations helix     # preview a specific spinner
npx unicode-animations --list    # list all spinners
```
Translation:
```bash
npx unicode-animations --web     # open browser demo
npx unicode-animations           # cycle through all in terminal
npx unicode-animations helix     # preview a specific spinner
npx unicode-animations --list    # list all spinners
```

Content:
## All spinners

### Classic braille

| Name | Preview | Interval |
|------|---------|----------|
| `braille` | `⠋ ⠙ ⠹ ⠸ ⠼ ⠴ ⠦ ⠧ ⠇ ⠏` | 80ms |
| `braillewave` | `⠁⠂⠄⡀` → `⠂⠄⡀⢀` | 100ms |
| `dna` | `⠋⠉⠙⠚` → `⠉⠙⠚⠒` | 80ms |

### Grid animations (braille)

| Name | Frames | Interval |
|------|--------|----------|
| `scan` | 10 | 70ms |
| `rain` | 12 | 100ms |
| `scanline` | 6 | 120ms |
| `pulse` | 5 | 180ms |
| `snake` | 16 | 80ms |
| `sparkle` | 6 | 150ms |
| `cascade` | 12 | 60ms |
| `columns` | 26 | 60ms |
| `orbit` | 8 | 100ms |
| `breathe` | 17 | 100ms |
| `waverows` | 16 | 90ms |
| `checkerboard` | 4 | 250ms |
| `helix` | 16 | 80ms |
| `fillsweep` | 11 | 100ms |
| `diagswipe` | 16 | 60ms |

## Custom spinners

Create your own braille spinners using the grid utilities:

## All spinners

### Classic braille

| Name | Preview | Interval |
|------|---------|----------|
| `braille` | `⠋ ⠙ ⠹ ⠸ ⠼ ⠴ ⠦ ⠧ ⠇ ⠏` | 80ms |
| `braillewave` | `⠁⠂⠄⡀` → `⠂⠄⡀⢀` | 100ms |
| `dna` | `⠋⠉⠙⠚` → `⠉⠙⠚⠒` | 80ms |

### Grid animations (braille)

| Name | Frames | Interval |
|------|--------|----------|
| `scan` | 10 | 70ms |
| `rain` | 12 | 100ms |
| `scanline` | 6 | 120ms |
| `pulse` | 5 | 180ms |
| `snake` | 16 | 80ms |
| `sparkle` | 6 | 150ms |
| `cascade` | 12 | 60ms |
| `columns` | 26 | 60ms |
| `orbit` | 8 | 100ms |

| `checkerboard` | 4 | 250ms |
| `helix` | 16 | 80ms |

| `diagswipe` | 16 | 60ms |

## Custom spinners

Create your own braille spinners using the grid utilities:

RowTranslations.originContent: Create your own braille spinners using the grid utilities:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Create your own braille spinners using the grid utilities:

Content:
`makeGrid(rows, cols)` returns a `boolean[][]`. Set cells to `true` to raise dots. `gridToBraille(grid)` converts it to a braille string (2 dot-columns per character).

## API

### `Spinner`
Translation:
`makeGrid(rows, cols)` returns a `boolean[][]`. Set cells to `true` to raise dots. `gridToBraille(grid)` converts it to a braille string (2 dot-columns per character).

## API

### `Spinner`

RowTranslations.originContent: `makeGrid(rows, cols)` returns a `boolean[][]`. Set cells to `true` to raise dots. `gridToBraille(grid)` converts it to a braille string (2 dot-columns per character).
RowTranslations.translatedContent: `makeGrid(rows, cols)` returns a `boolean[][]`. Set cells to `true` to raise dots. `gridToBraille(grid)` converts it to a braille string (2 dot-columns per character).

Content:
### Exports from `'unicode-animations'`

| Export | Type |
|--------|------|
| `default` / `spinners` | `Record<BrailleSpinnerName, Spinner>` |
| `gridToBraille(grid)` | `(boolean[][]) => string` |
| `makeGrid(rows, cols)` | `(number, number) => boolean[][]` |
| `Spinner` | TypeScript interface |
| `BrailleSpinnerName` | Union type of all 18 spinner names |

### Exports from `'unicode-animations/braille'`

Same as above — the main entrypoint re-exports everything from the braille module.

## License

MIT
